Airtable: archived 9 legacy records, created 7 missing venues,
backfilled 5 empty records. One-pager and email draft for team.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 3 bugs: webhook join accessor, deleteMembershipPlan auth, credit code club_id filter

- Webhook trainer/course emails: Supabase join returns object not array, so .clubs?.[0] and .courts?.[0] always returned undefined (emails defaulted to 'de' lang and 'Court' name). Fixed with direct property access.
- deleteMembershipPlan: was missing all auth checks — any logged-in user could delete any club's plan. Added owner/super-admin check.
- createBooking credit code: was querying credit_codes without club_id filter, risking cross-club code collisions. Added .eq('club_id', club.id) to both read and update queries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve ~30 issues from round 3 triple-reviewer audit

Suppression system:
- Fix suppression leakage: add endLine bound for symbol-level suppressions
- Fix @aimd-ignore-next-line using getStartLine() instead of getEndLine()
- Support @aimd-ignore in inline comments (// and /* */) inside method bodies
- Wrap DateTimeImmutable in BaselineLoader with try/catch

Complexity metrics:
- Fix cognitive complexity false recursion (check receiver, not just method name)
- Fix cognitive complexity else nesting bonus (else gets +1, no nesting per SonarSource)
- Fix NPath ignoring if/elseif/switch condition expressions
- Add ArrowFunction handling to CyclomaticComplexity and NPath visitors

Other metrics:
- Fix CBO formula: use set union instead of Ca+Ce (per Chidamber & Kemerer)
- Fix DIT: track use-imports for correct parent class resolution
- Fix RFC: dedup instance method calls by receiver+method, not just method name
- Fix LocCollector: inline comments no longer subtracted from LLOC
- Skip TCC/LCC metrics for interfaces (meaningless without method bodies)

Analysis & Configuration:
- Fix DependencyVisitor: reset imports between namespace blocks
- Fix file deduplication when overlapping paths provided
- Fix --disable-rule to merge with YAML disabled_rules (not replace)
- Throw on cyclic derived collector dependencies instead of silent fallback
- Remove dead $providers code in CompositeCollector
- Wire RuleOptionsFactory.setConfigFileOptions() in RuntimeConfigurator

Infrastructure & Reporting:
- Security: add allowed_classes=false to SqliteStorage unserialize calls
- Fix SARIF: omit locations for project-level violations (schema compliance)
- Fix SARIF: use actual max severity for rule defaultConfiguration.level
- Relativize paths in Checkstyle and JSON formatters
- Fix GitClient.isRepository() for git worktrees (.git as file)
- Fix CacheKeyGenerator: use pathname fallback instead of empty key
- Fix GitScopeFilter: recognize bracketed namespace syntax
- Fix Profiler: enforce LIFO on out-of-order span stops

Rules:
- Fix AbstractCodeSmellRule: per-occurrence message and metricValue=1
- Fix legacy defaults mismatch (Cognitive error 25→30, NPath error 500→1000)
- Fix ClassNpathComplexityOptions: camelCase properties, default 200→500
- Add dual camelCase/snake_case support in multiple Options::fromArray()

Documentation:
- Add by-design comments to refuted findings (threshold convention, PMD formula,
  php-parser relative names, TypeCoverageOptions.getSeverity())

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix tile cache in /crm/sw.js — bust stale 503 opaque responses

The actual active SW is /crm/sw.js (not /service-worker.js).
It was caching opaque 503 error responses from Esri as valid tiles
because no-cors mode makes ALL responses opaque, passing the cache
check. Tiles served from cache were blank/grey forever.

Fix: bump TILE_CACHE to v2 (purges bad cache), bump CACHE_NAME to
v8 (forces SW update). Also improve tileCacheFirst to try cors first
(OSM supports it) so we can validate response.ok before caching,
falling back to no-cors only for servers that need it (Esri).

commit.message:
Encyclopedia enrichment: +110 screenwriting refs, 2989 meta summaries rewritten

- Add ScreenCraft, MasterClass, Save the Cat references for 71 story terms,
  11 theorists, and 28 writer figures
- Rewrite all 2,989 meta-style summaries ("Wikipedia's article on X") across
  9 dictionaries to describe concepts directly from entry intro text
- Fix cross-contamination where archive/mythouse content leaked into other
  source labels, deduplicate identical text across sources
- All 2,127 tests pass across 51 suites

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(node): wire NWTN inference, capability discovery, and gossip persistence

Compute Provider → NWTN Orchestrator wiring:
- ComputeProvider._run_inference() now dispatches to NWTNOrchestrator via
  BackendRegistry (Anthropic/OpenAI/local) when an orchestrator is injected
- ComputeProvider._run_embedding() wires to RealEmbeddingAPI when available
- node.py passes both orchestrator and embedding_api to ComputeProvider on
  initialization; graceful mock fallback when no API keys are configured
- Closes the P2P AI inference loop: remote nodes now get real LLM responses

Capability-based peer discovery:
- PeerInfo extended with capabilities[], supported_backends[], gpu_available
- PeerDiscovery.find_peers_with_capability() and find_peers_with_backend()
  enable compute job routing to capable nodes instead of broadcast-to-all
- New GOSSIP_CAPABILITY_ANNOUNCE subtype propagates node capabilities on
  startup and on backend configuration change
- capability_detection.py auto-detects available backends from env vars
- ComputeRequester prefers capable peers when routing inference/embedding jobs

Gossip persistence for late-joining nodes:
- GOSSIP_DIGEST_REQUEST/GOSSIP_DIGEST_RESPONSE message types added
- On new peer connection, node sends digest of last-seen timestamps per subtype
- Remote peer queries SQLite gossip log and sends catch-up messages
- Per-subtype retention: 1h for tasks/queries, 24h for content/agent ads
- Periodic cleanup prunes messages past retention window

Tests: 58 new tests across 3 files (12 NWTN integration, 24 capability
discovery, 22 gossip persistence) — all passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve code quality: fix go-github version, silent errors, and duplicate constants (#55)

## Summary

- Upgrade `go-github` from mixed v82/v83 to v83 only
- Surface silent errors in `org show` as stderr warnings
- Deduplicate repeated error message strings with per-package constants
- Add `captureOutput` test helper to `internal/cmd/org`

## What's Changed

### Bug Fixes
- **go-github v82/v83 conflict**: Changed import in
`internal/client/github.go` from v82 to v83 and removed v82 via `go mod
tidy`
- **Silent error in `org show`**: Errors from subscription/entitlements
fetch were silently ignored with `_`; now emitted as `warning: ...` to
stderr

### Improvements
- **Error message constants**: Deduplicated `"organization is required:
..."` / `"workspace is required: ..."` string literals by defining `var
err = errors.New(...)` in each package's `common.go`
- **Test helper**: Added `captureOutput` helper in
`internal/cmd/org/helpers_test.go` and used it in new tests

### Tests
- `TestOrgShow_ReadSubscriptionError_StderrWarning`: verifies warning
message on stderr when subscription fetch fails
- `TestOrgShow_ReadEntitlementsError_StderrWarning`: verifies warning
message on stderr when entitlements fetch fails

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Astro migration with dynamic theme system (#5)

* feat: Complete React-to-Astro migration with cleanup and enhancements

Migrate entire portfolio from React/Vite to Astro SSG with Tailwind CSS.
Remove all React dependencies, components, and configuration. Add services
page with contact dialog, bento grid skills layout, reusable Logo and
SocialLinks components, NDA notice, smooth scroll, and ESLint/Prettier
setup for Astro.

- Remove React, Vite, and all related deps/config
- Delete old .tsx components, contexts, hooks, and icon library
- Add Astro pages (index, services) with static .astro components
- Add contact dialog (native <dialog>) for services page CTAs
- Add bento grid layout for skills section (4-col desktop)
- Merge blockchain + smart contracts into single service
- Fix navbar border transition flash (border-transparent)
- Fix experience timeline order (Delivery Lead before Stellar)
- Remove hardcoded projects section
- Create reusable Logo.astro and SocialLinks.astro components
- Fix all type-only imports for verbatimModuleSyntax
- Configure ESLint with astro plugin, clean lint + format
- Remove unused packages (nanostores, clsx, tailwind-merge)
- Clean up empty directories and dead code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Delete index.html, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.app.json, tsconfig.node.json
leftover from the React/Vite setup. Add .astro and .vercel to .gitignore
as they are build artifacts.

commit.message:
refactor: extract ReviewQueueView hooks (Phase 3)

Extracted state management from ReviewQueueView into reusable custom hooks
following Single Responsibility Principle.

Changes:
- Created frontend/src/hooks/useReviewQueue.ts (131 lines)
  - Manages extraction list, stats, pagination
  - Handles filtering (min score, only flagged)
  - Provides loadMore, refresh, applyFilters methods

- Created frontend/src/hooks/useSelection.ts (62 lines)
  - Generic multi-select state management
  - Provides toggleSelection, selectAll, clearSelection
  - Reusable in any view with multi-select

- Created frontend/src/hooks/useReviewDialog.ts (119 lines)
  - Manages review dialog state (open/close, notes, decision)
  - Handles single and batch review submission
  - Integrates with notification system

- Refactored frontend/src/views/ReviewQueueView.tsx (485 → 411 lines, 15% reduction)
  - Replaced 11 useState variables with 3 custom hooks
  - Removed 180+ lines of business logic
  - Simplified handlers to call hook methods
  - UI rendering unchanged

- Fixed frontend/src/views/EditSourceView.tsx
  - Corrected syntax error in error handler

- Fixed frontend/src/components/EntityTermsManager.tsx
  - Removed duplicate import statement

Benefits:
- Single Responsibility: Each hook manages one concern
- Reusability: useSelection can be used in any multi-select view
- Testability: Hooks can be unit tested independently
- Maintainability: Component focused on UI, logic in hooks
- Type Safety: Full TypeScript interfaces for all hooks

All functionality preserved, no breaking changes.

Part of Phase 3 maintainability roadmap (MAINTAINABILITY_REVIEW.md).

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix video 0:00: prepend API_BASE to /videos/ paths in all video components

Root cause: the client (Vercel) and server (Railway) are on different
origins. <video src="/videos/file.mp4"> was resolving to the Vercel
origin, which has no videos - only the Railway server does.

The fetch() auth interceptor in api.js only rewrites /api/* paths to
the Railway origin. Static asset paths like /videos/* were untouched,
so the browser fetched from the wrong host entirely.

Fix: prepend API_BASE (VITE_API_URL) to all localPath values before
setting them as video src or download href in VideoGenerator,
VideoGallery, and QuickVideoButton.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Improve upload UX and raise file size limit to 2 GiB

- XMLHttpRequest-based upload with real progress bar and status card
- Distinguishes upload failure from "saved but not injected" errors
- Explicit 2 GiB maxFileSize/maxTotalFileSize in Formidable config
- 413 error response with human-readable message for oversized files

commit.message:
Fix memory exhaustion on Render free tier (512MB)

Root causes and fixes:

1. ml_signals.run_all(): downloaded 6mo OHLCV DataFrame per ticker and
   never freed it. Add explicit del + gc.collect() after signal extraction.

2. price_data.bulk_fetch(): held the large multi-ticker yf.download()
   DataFrame in memory for the entire duration of parallel .info fetches.
   Add del data + gc.collect() immediately after OHLCV extraction.

3. market_scanner scan_market(): Semaphore(10) meant 10 concurrent scans
   each holding info dict + ml_signals DataFrame simultaneously. Reduced
   to Semaphore(3). Also add gc.collect() after scan completes.

4. Scheduler job pile-up: watchlist, sectors, breadth, and market snapshot
   all fired at the same minute. Stagger them with 2/4/6/8 min offsets.
   Also reduce watchlist refresh from every 5 min to every 10 min —
   yf.download() is too memory-intensive to run every 5 minutes.

5. Add gc.collect() after the two heaviest scheduler jobs (watchlist
   refresh and scanner report generation).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: daily QA cleanup agent — front-end verification pipeline (#141)

* feat: daily QA cleanup agent — front-end verification pipeline

Add a 3-task Trigger.dev pipeline (qa-orchestrator → qa-browse →
qa-analyze-and-fix) that runs daily at 6am UTC. Browses pictures.london
with Playwright, compares against DB state, uses Gemini to analyze
discrepancies, and auto-fixes verified issues with full audit trail.

Detects stale screenings, broken booking links, TMDB mismatches,
missing Letterboxd ratings, and time mismatches. Classifies issues
as spot vs systemic (e.g., all BFI links broken → cinema-level
critical alert). DRY_RUN=true by default for safe rollout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update front-end extractor selectors to match pictures.london DOM

The original selectors assumed <time> elements, <section> tags, and
JSON-LD ScreeningEvent data that don't exist on pictures.london. Updated
to match the actual structure: div.bg-background-secondary cinema cards
with div.w-28 date/time containers and .text-accent-highlight for times.

Also adds scripts/qa-dry-run.ts for local testing without Trigger.dev.

Dry run results: 198 films, 4019 screenings extracted (was 0 before fix).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address 5 code review findings in QA pipeline

1. Flag-only fixes (broken links, wrong film) no longer claim applied:true
2. Completeness guard uses filmId instead of constant string in Set
3. TMDB mismatch detection uses cinema+title+datetime instead of bookingUrl join
4. Metadata key aligned: currentMatchConfidence in both analyzer and verifier
5. Added time_mismatch detection (Step B3) comparing FE vs DB datetimes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add Regent Street Cinema to registry (unblocks Trigger deploy)

The scraper existed but the cinema wasn't registered, causing import
errors during Trigger.dev deployment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: parse relative datetime strings from pictures.london

Production output showed all 2641 screening datetimes come as relative
strings ("Today 11:00", "Thu 12 Mar 17:40") not ISO dates. Added
parseRelativeDatetime() to convert these before time_mismatch detection
and TMDB mismatch datetime proximity matching.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: split QA orchestrator for reliable API dispatch + resilient audit trail

- Split qa-orchestrator into qaPipeline (regular task) + qaOrchestrator
  (cron wrapper) so API triggers dispatch reliably via Trigger.dev
- Made insertAuditRecord non-fatal — audit trail DB errors no longer
  crash the pipeline (root cause: data_issues table missing in prod)
- Admin API now triggers qa-pipeline directly instead of qa-orchestrator
- Exported titleToSlug from enrich-letterboxd for reuse

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------
